outlook.headline: LiDAR SoC demand is broadening on FMCW and ToF adoption
outlook.summary: Reads are uniformly constructive, with indie Semiconductor seeing hot demand as embodied AI and FMCW adoption lift need for LiDAR/radar processors and perception software, while SMIC flags automotive LiDAR-linked ToF as a supported application area. The next 1-2 quarters look better for LiDAR SoCs as content ramps across both perception processing and timing/ranging use cases, though the mention trend is still only modestly positive.

drivers.label: FMCW adoption lifts processing content
drivers.detail: Growing FMCW use is increasing demand for LiDAR/radar processors and perception software.

drivers.label: Embodied AI expands perception demand
drivers.detail: AI-driven sensing architectures are creating more demand for LiDAR SoCs tied to perception and fusion.

drivers.label: Automotive LiDAR ToF remains a supported end market
drivers.detail: SMIC’s read points to ToF products tied to automotive LiDAR as an active application area.

drivers.label: Mentions are trending slightly higher
drivers.detail: Company mention trend is still low but positive, suggesting gradual broadening rather than a sharp surge.

risks: Adoption remains early-cycle: FMCW and embodied AI are positive, but still need conversion into volume shipments.
The breadth of support is limited in the reads, so a single customer/program delay could soften near-term momentum.
If automotive LiDAR demand slows, SMIC’s ToF-linked support could prove less durable.
